risks.title: SQL injection via string concatenation
risks.description: Embedded SQL is constructed via string concatenation without parameterization.
risks.impact: Data exfiltration, unauthorized modification of banking records.
risks.mitigation: Move to parameterized queries/ORM; add input validation and query allow-lists.

risks.title: MAX(id)+1 key generation race condition
risks.description: IDs generated using MAX(id)+1 patterns; unsafe for concurrent usage.
risks.impact: Duplicate primary keys, inconsistent ledgers if multiple tellers.
risks.mitigation: Use DB identity/sequence; enforce unique constraints; add transactional inserts.

risks.title: Global shared ADODB recordsets in Common module
risks.description: Shared recordsets and global connections used across forms; hidden coupling.
risks.impact: Hard-to-reason side effects; inconsistent state during migration.
risks.mitigation: Introduce per-request/per-unit-of-work data access; dependency inject connections.

risks.title: PII handling and auditability gaps
risks.description: CustomerService touches PII fields; logging/audit coverage uncertain.
risks.mitigation: Define PII classification; add redaction and audit trail for reads/writes.

risks.title: Reporting parity and legacy DataReports equivalents
risks.description: Legacy reporting may rely on DataEnvironment/DataReport bindings.
risks.impact: Stakeholder disruption if reports change layout/values.
risks.mitigation: Inventory reports; define parity (pixel-perfect vs semantic); select replacement (SSRS/PowerBI/Crystal/etc).

risks.title: RBAC and admin authorization ambiguity
risks.description: Role/permission model exists but enforcement patterns vary by form.
risks.mitigation: Extract access control rules; implement centralized authorization middleware; add tests.
